questionUpdates.newEvidence: Week of Mar 3-7: Pentagon supply chain risk designation is having cascading real-world effects. CNBC reports (Mar 4) that defense contractors including Lockheed Martin are actively removing Claude from their supply chains. J2 Ventures says 10 portfolio companies have backed off Claude for defense use cases. Meanwhile, a leaked Dario Amodei internal memo (Mar 5) explicitly blamed Anthropic's refusal to 'pander to Trump' — stating they haven't donated to or praised the president, unlike OpenAI. Paradoxically, Claude is now the #1 free app on the US iOS App Store (AppFigures, Mar 6-7), breaking daily signup records globally — a textbook Streisand effect. Anthropic argues (citing 10 USC 3252) that Hegseth lacks legal authority for the designation. The situation remains in limbo: no official legal action filed by either side.

notes: Scanned The Verge, CNBC, Ars Technica, Sources (Alex Heath), AppFigures. Other stories considered but did not meet timeline bar: OpenAI developing a GitHub rival (early stage, months away — not an event yet); OpenAI Codex Security launch (product update); AI Wikipedia translation hallucinations (interesting but not historically significant); OpenAI delaying ChatGPT 'adult mode' (product timing). The Anthropic/Pentagon saga continues to evolve but the base event (Feb 28 designation) is already on the timeline — current developments are evidence accumulation, not a new inflection point. Note: web_search was unavailable (Gemini API key invalid), so scanning was done via web_fetch on major news sites.
